designerParts.name: Ball
designerParts.description: Just a simple ball.

designerParts.name: Beacon Light
designerParts.description: Small blinking lights.

designerParts.name: Block
designerParts.description: Just a simple block.

designerParts.name: Camera
designerParts.description: Attach to your craft to add new views that you can cycle through during flight.

designerParts.name: Cargo Bay
designerParts.description: Cargo bay that can hold stuff and comes with two hinged doors by default.

designerParts.name: Small Bay
designerParts.description: Cargo bay that can hold stuff and comes with a single sliding door by default.

designerParts.name: Command Chip
designerParts.description: Compact and lightweight alternative to using a Space Capsule for controlling a craft.

designerParts.name: Command Disc
designerParts.description: A pack combining a command chip and space to have some batteries and gyroscopes to have all you need to control your craft in a single, more configurable part.

designerParts.name: Command Pod
designerParts.description: Controls the craft. Every rocket must have at least one command pod. This command pod has a built-in gyroscope for attitude control. Batteries are included.

designerParts.name: Komodo Command Pod
designerParts.description: Space capsule which includes a small amount of fuel and built-in engines.

designerParts.name: Patriot Capsule
designerParts.description: Space capsule that has a bit more leg room than the alternatives. Great capsule if you love your country and if you have the money.

designerParts.name: Space Capsule
designerParts.description: Controls the craft. Every rocket must have at least one capsule or command chip. Includes built-in gyroscope for attitude control. Batteries are included.

designerParts.name: Vroz Space Capsule
designerParts.description: Controls the craft. Every rocket must have at least one capsule or command chip. This capsule has a built-in gyroscope for attitude control. Batteries are included.

designerParts.name: Vroz Orbital Module
designerParts.description: Spherical space capsulse that fits quite nicely on top of the Vroz Descent Module in case you want to bring more people into space.

designerParts.name: Space Habitat
designerParts.description: A fuselage capable of holding crew inside, but with a very low density. Ideal for large vehicles such as spaceships, space stations and the like.

designerParts.name: Crew Compartment
designerParts.description: A fuselage capable of holding crew inside with a high density of seats, but with a limited pressure range to work. If under too much or too little pressure, it will take damage.

designerParts.name: Interstage
designerParts.description: Use this to split your craft into stages.

designerParts.name: Side Interstage
designerParts.description: Interstage that is designed to connect to the side of a fuel tank, but can connect to other places, too.

designerParts.name: Docking Port
designerParts.description: Allows connecting two crafts in flight so they can be friends and transfer fuel.

designerParts.name: Electric Motor (old)
designerParts.description: Motor that can continuously rotate parts. Uses Electricity.

designerParts.name: Electric Motor
designerParts.description: Motor that can continuously rotate parts. Uses Electricity.

designerParts.name: Mage I Engine
designerParts.description: Powerful engine that can be used for takeoff and orbiting. Not a great choice for landing on moons.

designerParts.name: Pixie I Engine
designerParts.description: Lower power engine useful for second stages and landing on small planetary bodies.

designerParts.name: Apex I Engine
designerParts.description: It's heavy, expensive, and drinks RP-1 like there's no tomorrow, but it will have the power to get even your most poorly engineered contraptions into space.

designerParts.name: Tourist
designerParts.description: They pay money to go places, but sadly they lack jetpacks and grappling hooks, how useless...

designerParts.name: Drood
designerParts.description: A mysterious being that resembles an Earthling. They can be a bit fragile but are great at exploring and piloting. You can put them into cockpits, cabins or seats to control your crafts even when out of power, and get them out in flight to have a walk.

designerParts.name: Chair
designerParts.description: Astronauts will stand as long as you ask them to, but sometimes it's nice to give them a break and let them sit down.

designerParts.name: Fairing
designerParts.description: Protects precious cargo on its way to space.

designerParts.name: Fairing Base
designerParts.description: The base of your fairings. When using fairings, this must be the first part you connect to your craft.

designerParts.name: Fairing Nose Cone
designerParts.description: Nose cone specially designed to only connect to fairings.

designerParts.name: Fin
designerParts.description: Smaller wing section designed for rockets.

designerParts.name: Fuel Adapter
designerParts.description: Makes it easier to connect engines to odd places on fuel tanks.

designerParts.name: Fuel Tank
designerParts.description: Versatile fuel tank that can be resized into many shapes and can hold many different types of fuel.

designerParts.name: Battery Pack
designerParts.description: A properly sized and more handy fuel tank preset to have battery in it, in case you don't feel like using the built in battery of a command disc.

designerParts.name: Battery Tank
designerParts.description: If you don't worry about weight and you don't feel like bringing some solar panels with you, this clearly over-sized battery will do.

designerParts.name: Fuel Cell
designerParts.description: A generator capable of turning fuel into power. It is far from efficient burning it, but it can be a lighter and more compact alternative to large batteries.

designerParts.name: RTG
designerParts.description: Radioisotope Thermal Generators can be treated as a passive source of power thanks to their insanely radioactive plutonium core. With this you can have power anywhere in the system, but it doesn't come for cheap.

designerParts.name: Gyroscope
designerParts.description:
Allows attitude control without thrust. 
Uses electricity.

designerParts.name: Heat Shield
designerParts.description: Provides protection from intense heat during re-entry.

designerParts.name: Hinge Rotator
designerParts.description: Small motor that can rotate parts around a hinge.

designerParts.name: Inlet
designerParts.description: Inlets suck in air for jet engines. Make sure the front of the inlet isn't blocked by other parts, because that can obstruct its airflow.

designerParts.name: Ion Engine
designerParts.description: Extremely low power, high-efficiency engine that expels ions at nearly 50km/s. Requires electricity.

designerParts.name: Pegasus Engine
designerParts.description: High-bypass jet engines are the most fuel efficient style of jet engines, and are more commonly found on passenger airplanes where money matters.

designerParts.name: Griffin Engine
designerParts.description: This is a low-bypass jet engine that trades fuel efficiency for more thrust. It also includes an afterburner, which significantly increases its thrust and also makes it totally rad.

designerParts.name: Minotaur Engine
designerParts.description: With a great balance between power and efficiency, mid-bypass turbofans are the perfect election for mid size planes and small and fancy private jets.

designerParts.name: Centaur Engine
designerParts.description: Turbojets are the oldest and most inefficient of the jet engines. These were designed before engineers knew that redirecting some of the air around the core was more fuel efficient and produced more thrust.

designerParts.name: Label
designerParts.description: Displays text. For labeling stuff, or whatever.

designerParts.name: Airplane Landing Gear
designerParts.description: Customizable landing gear.

designerParts.name: Small Landing Gear
designerParts.description: Customizable landing gear.

designerParts.name: Landing Leg
designerParts.description: Useful for landing without smashing your engine into the ground.

designerParts.name: Merlin Leg
designerParts.description: A sturdy set of legs useful for landing without smashing your engines into the ground, with great aero for how far they can deploy

designerParts.name: KIS Leg
designerParts.description: A very advanced and modern landing leg that will allow you to bring down the rockets of the future

designerParts.name: Piston Leg
designerParts.description: A small and simple landing leg, capable of slowing down the impact on moon landers

designerParts.name: Light
designerParts.description: Use these to help light your way.

designerParts.name: Multi-function Display
designerParts.description: Runs special Vizzy programs to display interactive widgets during flight.

designerParts.name: Nose Cone
designerParts.description: When used correctly, nose cones can reduce drag.

designerParts.name: Hemisphere
designerParts.description: Half a sphere. Two of these make a ball. Don't expect them to roll nice though, the collider is just a cone, sadly.

designerParts.name: Parachute
designerParts.description: With this you should hopefully touch down with a more gentle touch, but don't push them too far or they will break.

designerParts.name: Drogue Chute
designerParts.description: Activate these in an atmosphere to reduce your chances of rapid unplanned disassembly.

designerParts.name: Piston
designerParts.description: Push parts away from each other with a customizable hydraulic piston.

designerParts.name: Skyline Prop
designerParts.description: Your classic civilian small aircraft propeller, suited for the early crafts that require of well rounded performance for cheap.

designerParts.name: RCS Nozzle
designerParts.description: Reaction control nozzles help provide precise attitude control. Uses monopropellant.

designerParts.name: Multi Directional RCS
designerParts.description: Reaction control nozzle that can gimbal and provide far greater control than a standard RCS nozzle. Some say it can do the work of five normal RCS nozzles.

designerParts.name: Spirit Engine
designerParts.description: This engine is not about power, but about precision. Capable of small orbit and attitude adjustments, this engine is a must in any deep space mission.

designerParts.name: Gnome Engine
designerParts.description: As close as a deflating balloon you can get in terms of complexity, but also in terms of thrust.

designerParts.name: Pixie Engine
designerParts.description: Low power engine useful for second stages and landing on small planetary bodies. Its electric pump allows for a quick start and very low throttling, but it requires to have some batteries on-board.

designerParts.name: Dragon Engine
designerParts.description: Highly efficient nuclear thermal engine. Warning: Do not lick.

designerParts.name: Mage Engine
designerParts.description: Powerful engine that can be used for takeoff and orbiting. Not a great choice for landing on moons.

designerParts.name: Wyvern Engine
designerParts.description: A sophisticated methalox engine with great efficiency thanks to one of the most advanced pump systems ever seen.

designerParts.name: Titan Engine
designerParts.description: Powerful and efficient engine that uses hydrolox and staged combustion.

designerParts.name: Apex Engine
designerParts.description: It's heavy, expensive, and drinks RP-1 like there's no tomorrow, but it will have the power to get even your most poorly engineered contraptions into space.

designerParts.name: Goblin Solid Motor
designerParts.description: Sugar, spice and everything nice. The favourite toy of any rocket nerd.

designerParts.name: Ifrit Solid Booster
designerParts.description: Powerful and cheap, but inefficient. Cannot be shut down once ignited.

designerParts.name: Phoenix Solid Booster
designerParts.description: An extremely powerful solid rocket motor. Brute forces its usefulness through sheer power with no concern for efficiency whatsoever.

designerParts.name: Rotator
designerParts.description: Small motor that can rotate parts.

designerParts.name: Car Wheel
designerParts.description: Resizable wheels that have a built-in electric motor.

designerParts.name: Offroad Wheel
designerParts.description: Resizable wheels that have a built-in electric motor.

designerParts.name: Shock
designerParts.description: Shocks are useful when you want to soften impact with terra firma.

designerParts.name: Solar Panel
designerParts.description: Single, stationary solar panel that can be resized.

designerParts.name: Solar Panel Array
designerParts.description: Configurable solar panel array that stows away into a remarkably small space.

designerParts.name: Structural Panel
designerParts.description: It may look like a wing, but it has no wing physics simulation. These are good when you need a lot of wing like shapes, but don't care if they generate lift. They don't take as much processing power as a normal wing, so your design will run a little faster.

designerParts.name: Strut
designerParts.description: Resizable strut that can help bolster your crafts.

designerParts.name: Test Dummy
designerParts.description: Human like device that can be used to monitor the safety of your crafts.

designerParts.name: Toggle Switch
designerParts.description: Switches when toggled.

designerParts.name: Wing
designerParts.description: Large wing section designed with airplanes in mind.
